content.text:
Have you ever stirred sugar into lemonade and watched it disappear? That is called dissolving!

When one material mixes completely into a liquid and you can no longer see it, we say it dissolved. The liquid that does the dissolving is called a solvent. Water is the most common solvent on Earth.

🍬 Sugar dissolves in water — it seems to vanish, but it is still there! You can taste it.

🧂 Salt also dissolves in water — stir it in and it disappears into the water.

🪨 Sand does NOT dissolve in water — it sinks to the bottom. You can still see it.

🛢️ Oil does NOT dissolve in water — it floats on top and stays separated.

When something dissolves, it forms a mixture called a solution. Seawater is a solution of salt dissolved in water. When something does NOT dissolve, the pieces stay separate — like sand in water.

Remember: just because you can no longer see something does not mean it is gone — it is still in the water!

diagrams.alt: Diagram showing sugar dissolving in water vs sand not dissolving

experiment.instructions: Drag the sugar cube into the glass of water and see what happens!

combinations.resultLabel: The sugar dissolved! It mixed completely into the water. The water is now sweet, even though you cannot see the sugar.
The sand did not dissolve! It sinks to the bottom and stays separate from the water.

practiceQuestions.prompt: What does it mean when sugar 'dissolves' in water?

options.text: The sugar mixes completely into the water

practiceQuestions.hint: Think about what happened when you stirred sugar into the water in the experiment.

practiceQuestions.prompt: Which of these will dissolve in water?
practiceQuestions.hint: Think about which material you cannot see after mixing it into water.

options.text: A mixture where one material dissolves into another
A type of solid that cannot melt
A liquid that does not mix with anything
A gas that rises in the air

practiceQuestions.hint: Seawater is an example — salt dissolved in water.

questions.prompt: When salt dissolves in water, where does the salt go?

options.text: It disappears completely and is gone forever
It stays mixed in the water — you just cannot see it
It floats on top of the water

questions.prompt: Which of the following does NOT dissolve in water?
What is the liquid that dissolves other materials called?
Seawater is an example of what?

options.text: A solution of salt dissolved in water
Pure water with no other materials
Frozen water with salt on top

questions.prompt: What happens when you mix oil and water?

options.text: The oil dissolves into the water
They stay separated — oil floats on top
